content: ResequenceDefinition.reverse() guard — core/camel-core-model/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/model/ResequenceDefinition.java:266 throws IllegalStateException("reverse() only supported for batch resequencer") when streamConfig != null; otherwise it defaults into batch mode (calls batch() if batchConfig == null) and sets batchConfig.setReverse("true"). In the model layer, reverse is a batch-only concept today.

DefaultExchangeComparator — core/camel-core-processor/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/processor/resequencer/DefaultExchangeComparator.java implements ExpressionResultComparator. Orders exchanges by a Long sequence number evaluated from an Expression: compare(o1,o2) = n1.compareTo(n2); predecessor(o1,o2) and successor(o1,o2) define stream adjacency between consecutive sequence numbers. The stream resequencer's ordering semantics live here, not in the EIP model.

Stream resequencer wiring — StreamResequencer (core/camel-core-processor/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/processor/StreamResequencer.java) takes a SequenceElementComparator<Exchange> in its constructor and delegates ordering to ResequencerEngine (same package, resequencer/ResequencerEngine.java). The comparator instance is created at reification time: ResequenceReifier (core/camel-core-reifier/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/reifier/ResequenceReifier.java) builds the StreamResequencer from StreamResequencerConfig (core/camel-core-model/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/model/config/StreamResequencerConfig.java).

Resequencer EIP model/catalog surface — resequencer model attributes are described in several places besides the java model: the catalog model json (catalog/models/streamConfig.json), the XML schemas (camel-spring.xsd, camel-xml-io.xsd), and the EIP docs page (docs/modules/eips/pages/resequence-eip.adoc) each carry the stream resequencer config independently.
